channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: ‘Piracy’: Will Trump’s 20 percent Hormuz toll find takers?
item.description: Trump says the US will start charging a cargo fee for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war: What is Europe’s new ballistic missile shield plan?
item.description: Nine European countries and Ukraine have launched a coalition to build Europe's own defence against ballistic missiles.

item.title: Indian activist Wangchuk urged to end hunger strike over exam paper leaks
item.description: Sonam Wangchuk has been​ fasting in New Delhi since June 28 in solidarity with Gen Z-led Cockroach Janta Party.

item.title: Mexico files criminal complaints over migrant deaths during ICE operations
item.description: Mexico files criminal complaints over migrant deaths during ICE operations

item.title: Which world leaders are visiting Qatar to pay their condolences?
item.description: Sheikh Hamad, who ruled the country from 1995 to 2013 and revolutionised modern-day Qatar, died on Thursday aged 74.

item.title: Iran lawmakers present Hormuz management bill as US war heats up
item.description: Hardline MPs table plan that aims to formalise control over the strait and undermine any negotiations.

item.title: Sheikh Hamad gave Arab media a voice
item.description: A tribute to the late Father Emir whose vision and resolve made Al Jazeera possible.

item.title: What’s behind the Sanaa Airport attack and latest Yemen tensions?
item.description: A diverted Iranian plane, a strike on Sanaa Airport and threats of retaliation have reignited tensions in Yemen.

item.title: WHO warns DR Congo Ebola outbreak may be double the official tally
item.description: UN health agency says cases are underreported while doctors at outbreak epicentre threaten to go on strike.

item.title: Israel launches new illegal settlement expansion plan
item.description: Israel launches new illegal settlement expansion plan

item.title: Russia readies to reroute exports from Sea of Azov after Ukrainian attacks
item.description: Ukraine says drones hit 11 Russian vessels in the Azov Sea, targeting tankers, dry cargo ships, and a tugboat overnight.

item.title: World Cup semifinals delay Barcelona’s, Real Madrid’s La Liga starts
item.description: From Lamine Yamal to Kylian Mbappe and Jude Bellingham, many Barca and Madrid stars feature in the World Cup semifinals.

item.title: World Cup: France, Spain unbeaten before semis, but only one can make final
item.description: Heading into semifinal, neither side has trailed in a match at FIFA World Cup, but only one can reach the 2026 final.

item.title: Sudan sentences RSF chief Hemedti to death: Who’s he, what’s he accused of?
item.description: Port Sudan trial of paramilitary leaders centres on atrocities in West Darfur.

item.title: What we know about UK politician Ann Widdecombe’s murder
item.description: The former politician was found dead in her home last week after sustaining 'serious injuries', according to police.

item.title: How new Yemen tensions could complicate the global energy crisis
item.description: If the fighting in Yemen expands, and the Houthis try to block Bab al-Mandeb, the world economy faces a new shock.

item.title: Tour de France crash and concussion cases expose limits of roadside checks
item.description: Three of the eight riders who have abandoned the Tour de France in the opening week have suffered concussions.

item.title: Lebanon, Israel hold talks in Rome on implementing framework deal
item.description: Beirut is hoping for progress towards securing an Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon.

item.title: Can Gulf countries defend themselves against renewed Iranian attacks?
item.description: Expensive interceptor missiles are facing mounting pressure from Iran's cheaply produced drones.

item.title: Dozens of healthcare workers in DRC strike amid Ebola outbreak
item.description: Healthcare workers at an Ebola treatment centre in the Democratic Republic of the Congo have gone on strike.

item.title: Schumer slams Trump over Iran war
item.description: Schumer slams Trump over Iran war

item.title: Utah Jazz guard Trey Alexander stretchered off court in NBA Summer League
item.description: Alexander, who signed a two-way deal with the Jazz last week. was injured in game against Chicago Bulls.

item.title: What is Iran’s Pickaxe Mountain, the mystery site Trump warns he’ll attack?
item.description: Tensions escalate as the US president threatens to destroy Iran's fortified nuclear site in the south of Tehran.

item.title: Argentina war vets urge focus on football for England World Cup semifinal
item.description: One of sport's great rivalries sees Argentina face England in an ever feisty encounter in the 2026 edition's semifinal.

item.title: ICE kills 26-year-old in Maine: What happened, and who else has ICE killed?
item.description: More than 60 people have died in ICE shootings or while in detention since Donald Trump returned to power.
